description_long: This is a military-style attack helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a bright orange color with black markings along the tail. The helicopter features a large, tinted cockpit canopy with multiple window panels, providing wide visibility for the crew. Its main rotor consists of four long, striped blades, while the tail section includes a smaller, two-bladed tail rotor mounted vertically. The landing gear comprises two robust skids extending from the fuselage, designed for stability on various surfaces. The helicopter's body is detailed with panel lines, access hatches, and a prominent nose section, likely housing avionics or weaponry. The overall design suggests a focus on agility and combat readiness, suitable for urban or tactical scenarios.
description: A bright orange, military-style attack helicopter with a large tinted cockpit, four main rotor blades, and sturdy landing skids. Black markings and detailed paneling accentuate its streamlined, combat-ready design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, wide cockpit canopy, and the full span of the main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit windows, and the main and tail rotors' alignment.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, rotor assembly, and the tail section with visible markings.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades with alternating green and white stripes, likely made of composite material, each blade extending nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-bladed tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored green and white, with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two straight, tubular landing skids made of metal, painted orange, with crossbars for structural support and stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows with a blue-green tint, framed in orange, wrapping around the front and sides of the cockpit for wide visibility.
